findings.evidence: `rg -n "fundamentalGroupMap_range_le_of_simplyConnected|existsUnique_continuousMap_lifts_of_range_le"` shows the TauCeti lemma at line 58 has no covering-map parameter, while Mathlib's adjacent lifting theorem `IsCoveringMap.existsUnique_continuousMap_lifts_of_range_le` is under `namespace IsCoveringMap` with `variable (cov : IsCoveringMap p)`.
findings.fix: Move it to the `FundamentalGroup` API and name it from the conclusion, e.g. `FundamentalGroup.map_range_le_mapOfEq_range_of_simplyConnected`, or add a real covering-map receiver if it is meant to be `IsCoveringMap` API.
findings.issue: `IsCoveringMap.fundamentalGroupMap_range_le_of_simplyConnected` is in the wrong namespace/name family: the statement has no `IsCoveringMap p` hypothesis and only proves `(map f a₀).range ≤ (mapOfEq p he).range`.

summary: One new range-inclusion lemma is placed and named as an `IsCoveringMap` API even though its statement has no covering-map hypothesis; the conclusion is purely `FundamentalGroup.map`/`mapOfEq` vocabulary.
